expectations.evidence: The second paragraph begins with 'In this paper, we address this gap by presenting...' — a classic filler opener.

The first paragraph explicitly states: 'Many state-of-the-art roof segmentation approaches rely on 2D rasterization of point cloud data---converting three-dimensional measurements into grid-based representations such as digital surface models or top-view intensity images before applying segmentation algorithms.'

'Manual annotation of rooftop geometry is both time-consuming and expert-dependent, and publicly available labeled datasets cover only a narrow range of urban morphologies, limiting generalization across cities and national mapping campaigns.'
Contributions mention 'the AHN3 national airborne LiDAR dataset across three structurally diverse Dutch cities---Rotterdam, Amsterdam, and Utrecht---encompassing more than 12,000 rooftops.'

The contributions are structured as a three-part bullet triad (i), (ii), (iii) that reads as a suspiciously balanced filler construction, e.g., 'an unsupervised 3D geometric clustering pipeline... (ii) a systematic large-scale validation... (iii) a publicly released processing workflow...'

The word 'robust' appears as an empty adjective in contribution (ii): 'a systematic large-scale validation demonstrating robust performance across diverse urban morphologies.' The word 'state-of-the-art' also appears in the first paragraph: 'Many state-of-the-art roof segmentation approaches.'
The output uses LaTeX formatting including \cite{example}, --- em-dash syntax, and ~ non-breaking spaces, confirming it is LaTeX-ready academic prose.
